rulings.Case Title: VALENCIA GARDENS HOUSING LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. TERRANCE HALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Application To Enter Judgment For Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 29, 2021 line 9. PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION TO ENTER JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION Hearing Required to address how alleged third-party claims of tenancy made in 2021 constitute Defendant's breach of stipulation re: vacating the premises in January 2022. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SYED ALI VS. BIG DADDY'S ANTIQUES SF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 29, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANTS BD ANTIQUES S.F., INC. AND STEVEN BROWN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Hearing Required to address why Raymond Graham should not be referred to California State Bar re: advice provided as to California law and procedures. Motion is GRANTED as to Defendant BD Antiques, SF. Inc per CCP 473(d). The review of the "judgment roll" reflects no proof of service for the service of summons and complaint and amendment to complaint after the order granting Amendment was requested (September 18, 2020) or entered (October 27, 2020) and before the default was entered on November 4, 2021. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that unfiled Doe Amendment (signed on September 8, 2020) served before leave of Court to file such Doe Amendment was requested (September 18, 2021) properly confers jurisdiction enabling the entry of default. Motion is DENIED as to Defendant Brown. Motion filed a year after the entry of default. At no time was San Francisco Superior Court completely closed for business; by September 2020 all filings were available to be viewed and made on-line; and the last emergency COVID-19 Order dated April 20, 2020 (prominently displayed on the Home page of the Court's official website) that could conceivably be interpreted as an order staying litigation expired by its own terms on June 1, 2020, 3 months before this action was filed. Eviction moratorium on its face has no applicability to breach of contract cases that do not seek possession. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANCHORAGE HOLDINGS, L.P., A DELWARE LIMITED VS. SUBWAY REAL ESTATE CORP.,A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment As To Defendant Alexander Usupova
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 29, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF ANCHORAGE HOLDINGS, LP'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT AS TO DEFENDANT ALEXANDER USUPOVA is GRANTED in part and DENIED without prejudice in PART. Motion is granted in the amount of $119,436.14 (base rent, additional rent and late charges through February 2021). Motion is otherwise denied without prejudice to be brought again with supporting evidence regarding mitigation of damages since February 17, 2021 and support for attorney's fees claim. To the extent Defendant presents a claim of exemption in opposition to this motion, the claim as denied as not supported by evidence. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: KDM, A JOINT VENTURE VS. ALI AHMED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 29, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF KDM, A JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and moot in part. Motion is GRANTED as to general denial with leave to amend. General denial only applies when the action is limited jurisdiction and the amount in controversy is less than $1,000. Complaint is filed as limited jurisdiction and alleges amount in controversy "exceed[ing] the sum of $10,000." Amended answer referenced in the ruling on the demurrer shall also include proposed amendment specifically addressing each paragraph in the complaint by admitting, denying, admitting portions and denying portions, or denying on the basis of lack of information or belief upon which to admit or deny. Motion is otherwise moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

PLAINTIFF KDM, A JOINT VENTURE'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to affirmative defenses. No later than November 29, 2021 by 9:00 a.m. Defendant to file a declaration attaching a proposed amended answer, including factually supported affirmative defenses listed in section II of the Answer to Complaint. rulings.Case Title: DALIN GOVEA VS. MARITZA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 29, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT MARITZA GARCIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to specify the amount of daily damages sought. The complaint as drafted suggest that the daily damages requested may be any amount of $29.71 or more per day and that the damages as of August 14, 2021(date of complaint) "exceed $25,000" (Complaint 3:26-27; note that present tense is used) presumably calculated from December 4, 2021 (Complaint 4:4). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
